title: Understanding Spring Stretch
text: The stretch of the spring is proportional to the weight it supports. This means that the more nails in the box, the more the spring stretches.

title: Initial and Final Stretch
text: At the beginning of the day, the spring stretches $$50$$ cm. At the end of the day, it stretches $$30$$ cm. This change indicates the number of nails used.

title: Calculate the Fraction of Nails Used
text: To find the fraction of nails used, calculate the difference in stretch and divide by the initial stretch.

title: Calculate the Difference in Stretch
text: What is the difference in the spring stretch from the beginning to the end of the day?

title: Calculate the Fraction
text: Divide the difference in stretch by the initial stretch to find the fraction of nails used.
